title: URL Allowlisting, Untrusted API Data, Terminal Safety, Files, and Resource Abuse
description: The client follows redirects anywhere, prints control characters from remote data, accepts arbitrary save paths, logs full responses, and lets crafted records exhaust memory or terminal output. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Revalidate scheme, host, port, redirect count, and destination policy on every hop and reject user-controlled egress.
Normalize or visibly escape control, bidi, and ANSI sequences from API, file, and user data before terminal presentation.
Choose application-owned storage roots, reject traversal or unexpected file types, and minimize permission and symlink assumptions.
